condensed_summary: AgentHub's pricing is undisclosed, but its three source markets converge on a per-user/per-employee-per-month (PEPM) value metric: LMS vendors price ~$6-11 PEPM (SAP SuccessFactors ~$6.30, Cornerstone ~$8-11, Docebo LMS ~$7-10), KM tools ~$8-30 PEPM, and AI-search/agent tools the premium tier (Glean ~$45-50+ base plus ~$15 AI add-on; Microsoft 365 Copilot $30 add-on). Inferred likely architecture: a Docebo platform-tier base license (PEPM) plus a consumption/AI-credit layer for agentic actions, mirroring the market's shift from seats to AI usage. The adjacent ecosystem is dense and dual-sided: Docebo integrates natively with 20+ knowledge sources (Confluence, SharePoint, Drive, Slack, Teams) and HRIS/CRM (Salesforce, ADP, BambooHR, Okta), while the MCP Server makes Docebo a knowledge source inside Claude/Copilot/ChatGPT, simultaneously creating reach and platform dependency on those AI assistants. Overall disruption risk is HIGH: Microsoft (Copilot + Viva Learning Agent), Glean ($7.2B, $200M ARR, 100M+ agent actions), and AI-native HR moves (Workday+Sana, Gloat Agentic HR, SAP Joule) are all converging on the same 'agentic knowledge + learning' space AgentHub is creating, while AgentHub's own AI agents are not live until fall 2026.

pricing_models.model: Tiered platform subscription, per-employee-per-month (PEPM), modular add-ons; quote-based / custom
pricing_models.price_points: Existing Docebo LMS ~$7-10 PEPM (~$84-120/user/yr), often a setup fee plus active-user fee; 1,000-2,000 users typically mid-five-figures annually, tens of thousands of learners reach six figures. Two primary tiers (Elevate, Enterprise). AgentHub list pricing NOT public (behind contact sales wall); pricing explicitly undisclosed in launch materials.
pricing_models.source: ITQlick Docebo pricing 2026 (itqlick.com/docebo-lms/pricing); educate-me.co Docebo pricing guide; product brief (AgentHub pricing undisclosed)

pricing_models.price_points: Enterprise $30/user/mo (annual) on top of qualifying base license (all-in ~$24-60+); Business $18/user/mo promo (std $21) for orgs up to 300 users; Copilot Studio priced in message/credit packs.

pricing_models.source: eesel AI / EPC Group / Velosio Copilot pricing 2026; CloudZero Copilot Studio pricing

dominant_value_metric.metric: Per-user / per-employee-per-month (PEPM) seat licensing, increasingly paired with a consumption/AI-credit layer for agentic actions
dominant_value_metric.evidence: 7 of 8 benchmarked vendors price primarily per-user/seat (Docebo, Cornerstone, SAP, Notion, Guru, Bloomfire, Microsoft Copilot base). The AI-native tier is shifting toward usage: Glean charges a separate AI add-on and references agent-action volume (100M+ agent actions/yr; 1B target), and Microsoft Copilot Studio meters by message/credit. This signals a market-wide migration from pure seats toward seat-plus-consumption for agentic AI.

pricing_spectrum.lowest: SAP SuccessFactors Learning ~$6.30/user/mo (review-inferred) and Notion Plus ~$10/user/mo (lowest self-serve)
pricing_spectrum.highest: Glean ~$45-50+ base plus ~$15 AI add-on (~$60+/user/mo); enterprise contracts $200K-$240K+ base, fully-loaded TCO up to ~$480K/yr
pricing_spectrum.typical_range: Core LMS/KM: ~$6-15 PEPM. AI-search/agent tier: ~$30-65 PEPM. Enterprise annual contracts: LMS ~$72K-six figures (per geography summary); AI-search ~$50K floor to $200K+.

findings.positioning_map: Three pricing strata: (1) Low-cost / self-serve KM (Notion, Guru, Bloomfire, ~$8-30 PEPM, freemium present). (2) Mid-priced enterprise LMS/HCM (SAP, Cornerstone, Docebo LMS, ~$6-11 PEPM, sales-led, no freemium). (3) Premium AI-native search/agents (Glean ~$60+ PEPM, Copilot $30 add-on, consumption-influenced). AgentHub should position in the premium AI-native tier on value (closed-loop learning+knowledge+skills+agentic AI) but can anchor price credibility to its existing LMS base. INFERRED likely architecture: a Docebo platform-tier base license (PEPM, bundling LMS + Enterprise Knowledge + Skills Intelligence) plus an AI-credit / agentic-consumption layer and per-source connector packaging - allowing land on platform seats and expand on AI usage, which both defends against per-seat-only LMS pricing pressure and matches the consumption direction set by Glean and Copilot Studio. This is INFERRED; Docebo has not disclosed AgentHub pricing.

gaps: AgentHub actual list pricing and packaging are undisclosed (behind contact sales wall); all AgentHub pricing here is INFERRED from category benchmarks.
No public detail on whether Docebo will meter agentic AI actions/credits or bundle them into platform tiers.
Regional (NA vs EMEA) price differentials for these vendors not separately published; LMS contracts avg ~$72K per geography summary but vendor-level EU pricing not isolated.
Per-user prices for SAP/Cornerstone are review-site inferred, not official price lists.

ecosystem_map.source: Docebo integrations marketplace; Microsoft Viva Learning content connectors (epcgroup.net)

platform_dependencies.dependency: MCP / AI-assistant ecosystem dependency: AgentHub's distribution via the MCP Server depends on Anthropic (Claude), Microsoft (Copilot), and OpenAI (ChatGPT) continuing to support third-party MCP knowledge sources. Microsoft and OpenAI are also competitors building native enterprise knowledge/learning agents, so they could deprioritize, gate, or out-compete external sources inside their own assistants.
affected_competitors: any KM/LMS vendor relying on being a knowledge source inside Copilot/ChatGPT
platform_dependencies.source: docebo.com/products/mcp; Microsoft Copilot Studio knowledge sources (learn.microsoft.com)

platform_dependencies.dependency: Microsoft 365 surface dependency: SharePoint/Teams are simultaneously key data sources, delivery surfaces, and the home of Copilot + Viva Learning Agent. Microsoft controls the data-access terms (Graph/Work IQ API) and the in-workflow surface where AgentHub wants to live.
platform_dependencies.source: Microsoft Work IQ API / Copilot Studio 2026 wave 1 (learn.microsoft.com); microsoft.com Copilot blog

platform_dependencies.dependency: HRIS system-of-record dependency: Skills Intelligence and permissions-aware knowledge rely on Workday/SuccessFactors data and permissions. These vendors are building competing native AI agents (Workday+Sana, SAP Joule, Gloat Agentic HR on Workday/Oracle/SAP), and could restrict or out-feature external skills layers.
platform_dependencies.source: Josh Bersin - Workday+Sana, Gloat Agentic HR (joshbersin.com)

findings.workflow_position: AgentHub sits at the convergence point of three stacks. Upstream/input: enterprise knowledge repositories (Confluence, SharePoint, Drive, Slack, Teams) and HRIS systems of record (Workday, SuccessFactors) feed it knowledge, roles, and permissions. Core: it turns that knowledge into learning content, maps it to skills, and applies agentic AI in a closed loop. Downstream/delivery: it surfaces in the flow of work (Teams, Slack, Salesforce) and, via the MCP Server, inside AI assistants (Claude/Copilot/ChatGPT). It therefore sits alongside - and partly competes with - both the LMS it grew from and the enterprise AI-search/assistant layer (Glean, Copilot).

gaps: Docebo's formal SI / consulting / implementation partner roster (named GSIs or boutique L&D consultancies) not enumerated in available sources (55 companies cited in Docebo partner ecosystem via Partnerbase, but breakdown by type unclear).
Whether AgentHub knowledge connectors are read-only or bidirectional, and data-residency handling per connector (relevant to EU AI Act/GDPR procurement) not detailed.
Revenue/strategic weight of channel vs direct in EMEA not confirmed.

risks.description: Microsoft folds the same value proposition into Microsoft 365 - Copilot for enterprise knowledge across SharePoint/Teams plus the Viva Learning Agent (in public preview) for personalized learning, grounded by the Work IQ API. A 'good-enough, already-bought' Microsoft bundle could absorb the AgentHub use case for the large M365-standardized installed base, especially in NA.
risks.evidence: Microsoft 365 Copilot ($30 add-on) is GA at scale; Microsoft launched a Learning Agent in public preview delivering AI-powered personalized learning; Copilot Studio 2026 wave 1 adds new knowledge types and agent governance; Work IQ API (public preview) exposes Copilot's org-grounded intelligence to custom agents. Viva Learning already integrates Cornerstone/SuccessFactors/Workday content.
risks.source: learn.microsoft.com Copilot Studio 2026 wave 1; microsoft.com Copilot blog; epcgroup.net Viva Learning

risks.description: AI-native enterprise knowledge agents (Glean) expand from search into the agentic, multi-source knowledge-graph platform layer AgentHub is creating - and could add learning/skills adjacencies faster than Docebo can ship its agents (not live until fall 2026).
risks.evidence: Glean doubled ARR to ~$200M in ~9 months at a $7.2B valuation (Series F June 2025), powers 100M+ agent actions annually targeting 1B by end of 2026, and has moved 'beyond search' with Agentic Engine 2 and Canvas co-authoring. It already covers the multi-source, permissions-aware knowledge graph AgentHub depends on.

risks.description: HCM suite owners build native agentic learning+skills, eliminating the need for a separate platform. Workday (with Sana), SAP (Joule agent across SuccessFactors), and skills-platform Gloat (Agentic HR) are all launching AI agents using the HRIS system-of-record and permissions - exactly the skills-intelligence layer AgentHub differentiates on.
risks.evidence: Josh Bersin (Mar 2026): Workday+Sana unveiled a new AI strategy; Gloat launched Gloat Agentic HR running in Copilot/Teams/Slack using Workday/Oracle/SuccessFactors rules; SAP Joule accesses SuccessFactors. These incumbents own the employee/role data AgentHub's Skills Intelligence needs.
risks.source: joshbersin.com Workday+Sana and Gloat Agentic HR articles; SAP Joule (search results)

risks.description: Rapid M&A and portfolio reshaping in corporate learning could compress the category before AgentHub's converged category is established, and large suite acquirers could bundle away point value.
risks.evidence: Coursera/Udemy consolidation activity (Jan 2026); Skillsoft selling Global Knowledge (May 2026) to refocus on an AI-native skills platform; Docebo itself acquired Zive and 365Talents. Active rollups signal a consolidating competitive set.
risks.source: openpr.com LMS market 2026; SEC Skillsoft 8-K FY2026 (sec.gov)

risks.description: Category-establishment risk: the converged 'learning + knowledge + skills with agentic AI' category is unproven and unsized, and AgentHub's own AI agents are not live until fall 2026. Buyers may default to category-native point tools (LMS, Glean, Copilot) rather than adopt a new converged category, leaving Docebo to evangelize a category competitors then capture.
risks.evidence: Stage 1 market-sizing: converged category Emerging/unsized; product brief: agents not live until fall 2026 and category not yet established. Meanwhile every adjacent vendor is shipping agentic features now.

risks.description: EU AI Act (HR uses classified high-risk; obligations from Aug 2, 2026) plus GDPR raise the governance/transparency/data-residency bar for an agentic system touching employee data across 20+ sources. This is more a barrier-to-adoption/cost than an obsolescence threat - and Zive's German roots (BSI C5, EU residency) can turn it into a differentiator.
risks.evidence: Stage 1 geography: EU AI Act HR-AI high-risk obligations Aug 2, 2026; GDPR data-residency/sub-processor disclosure now gating EMEA procurement; agentic three-in-one raises IT/Security/DPO bar.
risks.timeline: EU AI Act obligations from Aug 2, 2026
risks.source: Stage 1 geography summary (EU AI Act / GDPR)

risks.description: Open-source RAG/agent frameworks (LangChain, LlamaIndex, open MCP connectors) plus open LLMs let well-resourced enterprises build internal knowledge+learning agents over their own repositories, undercutting paid platforms at the high-IT-maturity end.
risks.evidence: MCP itself is an open protocol (Docebo exposes via MCP Server), lowering build-vs-buy friction. However, enterprise needs permissions-aware governance, HRIS/skills mapping, and L&D workflows that DIY rarely sustains - limiting probability for the 500+ employee target.
risks.source: docebo.com/products/mcp (MCP as open protocol); general framework landscape

big_tech_assessment.signals: Microsoft has the strongest convergence of signals: Microsoft 365 Copilot at enterprise scale ($30 add-on), a Viva Learning Agent in public preview for personalized learning, Copilot Studio 2026 wave 1 adding new knowledge types and governance, the Work IQ API exposing org-grounded intelligence to custom agents, and Viva Learning content integrations with Cornerstone/SuccessFactors/Workday. Microsoft owns the SharePoint/Teams surfaces and data that AgentHub both ingests and delivers into. Google is a weaker entrant in HR/L&D specifically (Gemini/Workspace lacks an L&D motion), and Salesforce is more CRM-adjacent (Agentforce) than learning-focused.
big_tech_assessment.source: learn.microsoft.com Copilot Studio 2026 wave 1; microsoft.com Copilot blog; epcgroup.net Viva Learning

findings.risk_summary: AgentHub is launching into a space being simultaneously attacked from three directions - big tech (Microsoft Copilot + Viva Learning Agent), AI-native challengers (Glean at $7.2B), and HCM incumbents adding agents (Workday+Sana, SAP Joule, Gloat) - while its own AI agents are not live until fall 2026 and the converged category is unproven. Docebo's defensibility rests on its installed LMS base, the closed-loop learning+knowledge+skills combination, the MCP distribution play, and Zive's EU-compliance edge; speed to GA and category evangelization are the decisive variables.

gaps: No confirmed public statement of Microsoft/Google explicitly targeting the 'knowledge-to-learning-content' use case as a named product (Viva Learning Agent is adjacent but not identical).
Glean has not publicly announced an L&D/learning-content product, so its move into AgentHub's exact lane is inferred from trajectory, not announced.
No detailed read on whether HRIS incumbents will restrict third-party skills-layer data access (the key dependency-to-disruption pivot).
Probability ratings for category-establishment risk rely partly on Stage 1 summaries rather than fresh primary buyer data.

metadata.scenario_emphasis_applied: new_product_launch: (Q10) deepened macro/agentic-AI and big-tech encroachment analysis - assessed Microsoft Copilot/Viva Learning Agent, Glean, and HCM-incumbent agentic moves (Workday+Sana, SAP Joule, Gloat) as the primary disruption vectors alongside AgentHub's own fall-2026 agent timeline and unproven category. (Q6) inferred AgentHub's likely pricing architecture (platform PEPM tier + AI-credit/consumption layer) by benchmarking LMS (PEPM ~$6-11), KM (~$8-30), skills, and AI-search (Glean ~$60+, Copilot $30 add-on) pricing, clearly labeling all AgentHub pricing as inferred since it is undisclosed. (Q7) mapped the dual-sided ecosystem: native knowledge/HRIS/CRM integrations as inputs and the MCP/AI-assistant ecosystem (Claude/Copilot/ChatGPT) as both distribution channel and platform-dependency risk.
